statement.p: Casandra and Sebastian are working together to paint a room. Casandra works 2.5 times as fast as Sebastian does. If they work together, it took them 10 hours to complete the job. Answer the following questions:
If Casandra paints the room alone, it would take her hours to complete the job.
If Sebastian paints the room alone, it would take him hours to complete the job.

solution.p: Lets understand how to use fractions to model work problems. Assume it takes a person 5 hours to complete a job. If we treat the whole job as one whole, each hour, this person would complete \frac{1}{5} of the job.
Similarly, if it takes a person x hours to complete a job, each hour, this person would complete \frac{1}{x} of the job.
Now we are ready to model this situation with math.
Assume that, if Casandra paints the room alone, it would take her x hours to complete the job. Each hour, Casandra does \frac{1}{x} of the whole job.

Since Casandra works 2.5 times as fast as Sebastian does, if Sebastian paints the room alone, it would take her 2.5x hours to complete the job. Each hour, Sebastian does \frac{1}{2.5x} of the whole job.
Its given that, if they work together, it took them 10 hours to complete the job. Each hour, they can complete \frac{1}{10} of the whole job.
Now we can write an equation and solve it:
In the second step, to get rid of all fractions, we multiplied each term with the common denominator {2.5\cdot 10x}.
By the solution we got, it would take Casandra {14} hours to complete the job.
Since Casandra works 2.5 times as fast as Sebastian does, it would take Sebastian 2.5\cdot{14}={35} hours to complete the job.
